title: Waltham police searching for girl, 17, missing since July 3
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Victoria 'Tori' Amore, 17, was last seen on July 3 leaving her home on Lexington Street in Waltham.
She departed in a dark sedan without her EpiPen or inhaler, crucial for her severe allergies and asthma.
Waltham police urge anyone with information on Amore's whereabouts to contact them at 781-314-3600.

title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth, Massachusetts.
The truck driver involved was cooperative with police; no charges have been announced yet, authorities said.
Massachusetts State Police and Dartmouth Police are investigating the crash, which follows recent bicycle fatalities in the state.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, allegedly assaulted a Burger King employee and customer, prompting charges of assault and battery and resisting arrest.
The incident occurred at 1 Maverick Square, East Boston, where Donovan punched a customer and resisted arrest at nearby MBTA Maverick Station.
Donovan pleaded not guilty, was released on personal recognizance, and is scheduled for a pre-trial hearing on August 7.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, was charged with murder and other offenses in the July 4 killing of Xavier Bautista.
Bautista, a Cambridge DPW worker, was shot near Broadway and Norfolk Street and found dead an hour later.
The incident has sparked debate over Cambridge's decision to discontinue the ShotSpotter gunshot detection system.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange after a parachute accident caused by a gust of wind.
This marks the second fatality at Jumptown Skydiving in a year; previously, a collision killed a Connecticut man.
Massachusetts State Police and the Orange Police Department are investigating, with the Medical Examiner determining cause of death.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's request to include testimony from other women with postpartum mental illness was denied by Judge William Sullivan.
Jury selection for Clancy's murder trial is set to begin on July 20, with an expected duration of four to six weeks.
Defense attorney Kevin Reddington plans to pursue an insanity defense, citing Clancy's struggles with postpartum mental illness.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranked 8th on ESPN's list of NFL's top-10 quarterbacks after finishing second in MVP voting.
Maye led the league in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage last season.
Maye helped the Patriots improve to a 14-3 record, leading them to a Super Bowl appearance against Seattle.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship, consuming 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Webb narrowly defeated 18-time Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest champion Joey Chestnut, who ate 7.54 pounds of wings.
Massachusetts native Geoff Esper, known for a Hooters wings record, finished third in the competition at The Brook, Seabrook, NH.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' linebackers were among the league's best in 2025, led by Harold Landry and Robert Spillane.
The team's defense ranked eighth in yards allowed per game and fourth in points allowed per game last season.
Rookie Gabe Jacas remains unsigned, while Elijah Ponder is expected to continue building on his impressive rookie year.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man was found dead after an apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph, on Sunday afternoon.
Randolph police and fire received a 911 call about a swimmer in distress at Great Pond around 4 p.m.
The incident is under investigation by State Police, with no further information available from the DA's office.
